channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Pollen Might Be Hurting Kids’ Test Scores
item.description: Researchers in Finland have found "robust evidence" of a link between pollen and worse academic performance.

item.title: Marathon is a different kind of extraction shooter, and it won’t be for everyone
item.description: We go hands-on with Marathon to find out whether it has what it takes to survive in the crowded live-service space.

item.title: Anker’s last-gen sleep buds are nearly 40 percent off ahead of daylight saving time
item.description: Bad news: most Americans are about to lose an hour of sleep next week. Good news: if you have trouble falling (or staying asleep), Newegg is currently selling Anker’s Soundcore Sleep A20 earbuds for $113.99 ($66 off) when you use coupon code MMSF88 at checkout, which drops them to just $6 shy of their lowest […]

item.title: Does Ticketmaster have a stranglehold on concert ticketing — or is it just ‘bringing joy’?
item.description: In a downtown Manhattan courtroom on Monday, lawyers for the US Justice Department and 40 state and district attorneys general warned a jury that the concert industry was being squeezed by a monopolist: Live Nation-Tickemaster. After amassing dominance over ticketing and artists' use of large amphitheaters, lead DOJ counsel David Dahlquist alleged, Live Nation constructed […]...

item.title: A suite of government hacking tools targeting iPhones is now being used by cybercriminals
item.description: Security researchers say exploits used by governments to hack into Apple iPhones have been found used by cybercriminals. They warned of an emerging market for "second hand" exploits.

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen tech lead steps down after major AI push
item.description: Reactions rippled through Alibaba's Qwen team after tech lead Junyang Lin stepped down following a major model launch.

item.title: MWC 2026 day 2: the 7 best gadgets we've seen today, from e-paper phones to tiny SSDs
item.description: We're still on the ground in Barcelona at the biggest mobile technology event of the year — here's what we've seen.

item.title: Elon Musk’s X Finally Tries to Stop the Epidemic of AI-Generated War Footage
item.description: It's unclear if other types of non-AI video will also be penalized.

item.title: Highguard has raided its last fortress, will shutdown on March 12
item.description: Highguard, the live-service multiplayer shooter that was announced at the Game Awards 2025, is shutting down on March 12. Developer Wildlight Studios shared that the game would be winding down alongside details of its final update, which include a new character, weapon and skill trees."Today we’re sharing difficult news. We have made the decision to permanently shut down Highguard on March 12," Wildlight Studios shared via a statement on the Highguard X account. "Since launch, more than two million players stepped into Highguard’s world. You shared feedback, created content, and many believed ...

item.title: The Biggest Movie of 2025 Is Finally Coming to Streaming
item.description: 'Zootopia 2,' which just squeaked by 'A Minecraft Movie' for the title, hits Disney+ next week.

item.title: Just three companies dominated the $189B in VC investments last month
item.description: Crunchbase data shows a record $189 billion of global venture capital flowed to startups last month, with AI startups nabbing 90% of the capital.

item.title: Feds Used Online Advertising Data to Track the Public’s Phone Locations
item.description: An internal DHS document reveals how data collected by the advertsing industry could be used for government surveillance.

item.title: TikTok down for some in US, thanks to second Oracle outage since sale
item.description: TikTok experienced a similar outage just days after ByteDance divested the app's U.S. operations.

item.title: Palantir CEO Uses Slur to Describe People Who Don’t Think the Government Will Take Their Company
item.description: Hello Alex this is your lawyer speaking. I am advising you today to please keep posting this shit.

item.title: Samsung is bringing glasses-free 3D to a billboard near you - 'spatial signage' could lead to a new generation of ads
item.description: Samsung launches glasses-free 3D Spatial Signage, combining optical depth technology, AI content tools, and large-format displays for commercial environments.

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon fight boosts Claude to No. 1 on app stores
item.description: After refusing to let its AI be used for autonomous weapons and mass surveillance, Anthropic has seen a surge in app downloads and paid subscriptions. Anthropic is making hay while the sun shines.

item.title: This HP OmniBook X is modern, portable, powerful, and on sale for just $650
item.description: Get a Grade A refurbished HP OmniBook X 14” (2024) for $649.99 — Snapdragon X Elite, 16GB RAM, 512GB SSD, and up to 26 hours of battery life.

item.title: Trump Wanted to Kill Congestion Pricing. Nevertheless, It Persisted
item.description: A federal judge just ruled against the Trump administration.

item.title: Netflix Hopes People Still Care Enough About ‘Squid Game’ to Tune into a Celebrity Reality Show
item.description: 'Squid Game: The Challenge' is getting its inevitable VIP edition.

item.title: Ratatan is marching its way into your console library on July 16
item.description: The indie gaming news has been flying out of Nintendo's event today at a rapid-fire pace. One of the announcements came from rhythm game Ratatan, which will release on July 16. The title will be arriving that day on the Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S. It's already been available on PC since September as an early access game. Unfortunately, readers may notice that the original Switch isn't on that list, which is not an oversight. "At this time, we have determined that the multiplayer experience on Nintendo Switch has not yet reached the quality level we aim to deliver," th...

item.title: AI companies are spending millions to thwart this former tech exec’s congressional bid
item.description: A tech billionaire-backed super PAC is spending $125 million to undercut candidates pushing for AI regulation. New York's Alex Bores, a former tech executive himself, is one of them.

item.title: What Trump’s war on Iran means for the US energy crunch
item.description: Fuel prices surged after the Trump administration launched strikes against Iran on Saturday, immediately raising questions about whether the war would increase energy costs for Americans, put more pressure on power grids, and push companies to pump out more oil and gas in the US. If conflict drags on, that could potentially play into Donald […]

item.title: Did Life Hitch a Ride to Earth? Scientists Smashed the Living Crap Out of Microbes to Find Out
item.description: The microbes "proved very hard to kill."
